title: crotalaria friesii
intro: Crotalaria friesii, also known as Fries's rattlebox, is a plant species in the legume family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves, seeds, and roots have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, Crotalaria friesii is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and it is also used in some African folk remedies for its perceived healing properties.
